top_weaknesses.issue: Abstract until line 27 — opening argument is conceptual; concrete industry examples (dental, construction) arrive late
top_weaknesses.fix: Promote a vivid example into the lede or first H2's first paragraph

top_weaknesses.issue: Strategy-deck/board-memo texture in places: 'The commercial implication is uncomfortable for incumbents', 'AI is not a feature category forever', 'durable advantage comes from trust, process, regulation, positioning, and integration'
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace memo lines with sharper operator language; say the mean thing about incumbents instead of cushioning it

top_weaknesses.issue: Claim 'Mature AI buyers are reducing agent vendor count and choosing one or two solutions per workflow category' is unsourced and reads as memo-speak
top_weaknesses.fix: Add citation/data point or rewrite with confident specifics

top_weaknesses.issue: Final H2 'The Agent Is The New Buying Center' is slightly broad
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen to name the buying behavior change concretely

consensus_summary: Real thesis with a coherent H2 spine and at least two genuinely shareable lines ('the database remains necessary, but necessity is not the same as strategic control'; 'the agent must go deep enough to complete work, not broad enough to sound intelligent'). Held back by a memo-y middle and late-arriving concrete examples.
